entry.content:
Good - I've disabled the broken Session Memory cron. Now I need to implement a replacement. Let me create a new cron that runs in the main session (systemEvent) and triggers memory saving.
Actually, systemEvent crons can't run tools - they just inject text. So I need a different approach. Let me create a simple cron that just reminds me to save memory manually, or I can implement proactive memory saving in the main session itself.
Actually, let me just update the memory with what's happened and note the solution.
